BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick analysis after the Cedarbrook Community Center hinted the weekend room rate for the chess academy might rise in March. Checking whether membership and drop-in income still covers the space and coaching costs.

CurrentState.Note: Does not include Eastwick Prep salary ($58K gross). This is side-income only.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if the community center raises the weekend room rate
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - room rate to $850/mo (center director said this is unlikely)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. The center director said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even on space and coaching costs alone is about $2,840/mo, roughly one weekend's room rental plus the assistant coach's pay. At 55 members there is comfortable headroom. Main risk is a seasonal enrollment dip, which usually recovers by the fall chess season.
ActionItems: Confirm the spring weekend room rate with the Cedarbrook Community Center
Check whether the room booking allows a 30-day cancellation without penalty
Consider raising membership dues $5/member at the next enrollment cycle
